TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Seediq, story: sdqNr-sisin_dakis 2020s

S.FORM: m mprengo ku manu di.
S.TRANSL: 嗯..我要說什麼呢？
Well, what should I say?

S.FORM: ma kari nii we bnhangan mu yaani wada maxan hari knkawas cbeyo di ha.
S.TRANSL: 我很久以前聽到這則故事。大約快十年了。
I heard about this story long time ago. It has been almost ten years.

S.FORM: a uka ka rudan nii uri di.
S.TRANSL: 這位族老已不在了。
This elderly has passed away.

S.FORM: e ba ciida na dah daha ka rudan tngesa yaku kari nii kingan we skpawan a sk a tiwas pawan kingan we sk a a pawan ma mesa ka rudan tsk cbeyo si we ita seediq si peni na meniq ta alang kesaun truwan peni e bale naq bale cbeyo uri.
S.TRANSL: 然後，族老說，聽說我們賽德克人以前住在所謂的Truwan地區。以前也真的是如此。
And the elderlies said it is said that we Seediq people used to live in the so-called Truwan area. It was really so in the past.

S.FORM: a klaali na ini kelai mesa a ini ta kelai ka karac han na.
S.TRANSL: 平時，我們不能預先知道天氣。
Usually, we do not know the weather beforehand.

S.FORM: mcuwin qmuyux ma mcuwin so m a malu karac peni.
S.TRANSL: 有時下雨有時好天氣。
Sometimes it rains, and sometimes it is fine.

S.FORM: ma ita cbeyo na uka ani mmaanu so saya ka bbrigan ta meniq alang a a a a seediq ta cbeyo.
S.TRANSL: 而且，以前我們賽德克部落裡，什麼現在的商品都沒有。
And, in the past, we did not have anything like today’s goods in our Seediq tribe.

S.FORM: ini so saya de kndalax ta alang de musa ta hori de musa ta mari ani mmaanu peni.
S.TRANSL: 那不像現在。現在我們從部落到埔里買各種東西。
It was unlike now. Now we go from our tribe to Hori to buy any kind of things.

S.FORM: kika asi naq mhuma naq so ppuqun kana so a a sama ma so kesaun na ma a bunga peni.
S.TRANSL: 所以，我們乾脆食物自己種植，例如蔬菜、地瓜等。
Thus we simply grew food by ourselves, for example, vegetables, and sweet potatoes.

S.FORM: kika eniqan a a kingan na manu di stexan sa a knkawas ciida peni hmnuwa naq uri a mndndengu ba karac paru.
S.TRANSL: 然後，一…聽說有一次，也不知怎麼搞的，那一年有一場嚴重的旱災。
Then, one... it is said that once, no one knows why, there was a really severe drought that year.

S.FORM: ma mndndengu ba karac paru ciida na.
S.TRANSL: 那是非常嚴重的旱災。
It was a really severe drought.

S.FORM: hmuwa ka huwa ka plaale han wa yaani wada kingan idas daha idas han we eniqan ka so ppuqun daha pnneniqan daha repun peni.
S.TRANSL: 起初還不嚴重。約一兩個月過去，他們倉庫裡還有食物。
At first, it was not serious. About one or two months later, there was still food put in their stockhouse.

S.FORM: ma kika ciida bale ba uka ppuqun di na laqi so pga so sgaalu di peni.
S.TRANSL: 那時候，真的沒有食物，那小孩就可憐了。
At that time, there was really no food, so hildren were pitiful.

S.FORM: deheya ka plaale so mhuqin e kingan ali de wada mgedang kingan ma mcuwin naq we kingan ali de wada mgedang daha.
S.TRANSL: 他們先死亡。每一天一個去世。然後有時候一天兩個去世。
They died first. Each day one passed away. And sometimes two passed away each day.

S.FORM: kiya ma uxe nii di mesa ka rudan ta si.
S.TRANSL: 我們的祖先想，這樣怎麼得了。
Our ancestors thought it could not be like this.

S.FORM: ma ha wu a ha ta hae ta theruy icin a yani.
S.TRANSL: 我們搬到另一個地方吧！
Let's go move to aother place!

S.FORM: so kii naq ka lngelungan na rudan cbeyo na.
S.TRANSL: 這就是祖先的想法。
This is the thought of the ancestor.

S.FORM: ye na hili mesa kesaun karac we anaq mndndengu karac ka hini we yaani ini kdengu karac ka icin kika ha ta theruy icin di mesa ka kari na.
S.TRANSL: 他認為即使這裡旱災，另一個地方可能沒有旱災。所以他說「我們遷徙到另一個地方吧」。
They thought even though there is drought here, maybe there is no drought in another place. Thus, he said, “Let’s migrate to another place.”

S.FORM: ma bale a kari na rudan we qitaun mu lngelungun mu saya we ma rih so maha wada mquri a yayung inu di suku ga lngelungun mu kari na rudan ciida.
S.TRANSL: 祖先的意思，現在我看來，是要朝Suku河前進。我想這是祖先當時的意思。
What the ancestor meant, now I think, was to go toward the Suku river. I think this is what ancestors meant.

S.FORM: ma lmngelung maha ta ba dehuk inu di mesa.
S.TRANSL: 然後，（他們）想，「我們會到哪裡？」
Then, (they) thought, “Where will we arrive?”

S.FORM: ma wada thuwe a mkkesa deheya ma bale ba uka naq riyung ppuqun ciida na.
S.TRANSL: 他們慢慢地走。當時真的幾乎已經沒有食物。
They walked slowly. At that time, there was barely any food.

S.FORM: irih so ani naq so m hei qhuni mmaanu odaan daha mekan elu uri di.
S.TRANSL: 路上經過的樹的果子他們都吃。
They ate all the fruits from trees they passed on the road.

S.FORM: ma ciida a ciida na a cbeyo cbeyo cbeyo cbeyo riyung ka kari nii hayan na.
S.TRANSL: 那時候，這口傳故事是則很久很久以前的故事。
At that time, this is a story of long long time ago.

S.FORM: ciida si na kana seediq daka c cmcamac peni na mgkela kari.
S.TRANSL: （傳述者說）在那個年代裡，人類與各種動物知道對方語言。
The storyteller said, at that time, people and all kinds of animals could know each other’s language.

S.FORM: kika m m mkleeluw mmaha s a skadi neniqun a a k neniqun daha knbra osaun daha k knbgurah meniq we smiling ka so qbheni ma so camac peni.
S.TRANSL: 所以在他們找要居住之地的路上、去開墾新地時，（他們）向飛禽走獸們請益。
Thus, on their way to find a new place to live, to go and reclaim a land, (they) consulted with fowls and beasts.

S.FORM: ma kika pneeruun ka pneeruun daha rih so mgd rih so m mg maanu di ado ka mggaluk kari ciida ka kesaun cmcamac daka seediq kika mkela mbahang uri ma mkela naq mprengo uri.
S.TRANSL: 先祖們就告訴了（牠們）。因為當時動物和人可以溝通，所以原來就聽得懂也會說語言對方的語言。
Ancestors told (them). Since at that time animals and people could communicate, they could understand and speak each other’s language.

S.FORM: ma ma mbahang so kiya ka camac de pgluwe namu bale mesa ka ima di camac peni a nasi huwa kesai so nii di mesa.
S.TRANSL: 牠們聽到那樣的事後，牠們說：「你們真不幸啊！你說這樣要怎麼辦？」
When animals heard about that, they said, “How unfortunate you are! Tell us how to handle this.”

S.FORM: ma ani si kari neyah truku ini ku kela ka kiya.
S.TRANSL: 太魯閣語如何稱呼我就不得而知。
I do not know how to call it in Truku.

S.FORM: ma dehuk me dehuk hii ka seediq ta kndalax truwan de kika manu di msseli hii kana ka cmcamac ma kana seediq.
S.TRANSL: 我們族人從Truwan抵達那裡。動物和人都聚集在那裡。
Our tribespeople came from Truwan and arrived there. And animals and people all gather there.

S.FORM: kelaan wada bbiyan bbiyan hari di peni wa mgrebu ceka na ali uri ini kelai.
S.TRANSL: 可能接近傍晚了，還是早上或中午？我不知道。
Maybe it was almost the evening, or was it in the morning or at noon? I do not know.

S.FORM: ma ma kika mesa ka manu di camac si hmuwa ka lmngelung a mprengo ka dangi rih so msdangi kana deheya nii han anaq s ani naq seediq ma ani naq camac wa.
S.TRANSL: 動物們說「是這樣做如何..」他們想，他們說他們都是朋友，即使是人和動物。
Then animals said, “How about…” (They) thought and said that they were all like friends, even though they were human and animals.

S.FORM: ani si bobo na egu riyung so pnrengoan daha we uka ka kesaun maha malu ka lm lmngelung seediq we uri mp mpmalu we ini daha kelai.
S.TRANSL: 之後有許多討論，但人類認為沒有可行的，他們也不知道結果好不好。
There was much discussion afterwards, but the human beings thought none was going to work out and they did not know whether the result would be good.

S.FORM: ma bobo na de so hari sisin ka rmengo di sa ke kesao ta kini ho mesa ka sisin si.
S.TRANSL: 之後，好像是繡眼畫眉說：「我們這樣做，好嗎？」
After that, maybe it was thrush who said, “Let’s do this way, shall we?”

S.FORM: yani kiya si maha malu so nodaan namu mesa ka ima di ma kndalax ciida di m maha malu uri wa mesa kana ka cmcamac ma kana seediq peni me mesa sa ima di duma so camac peni sisin ka kari na rudan wa kari na rudan a s tngesa yaku peni kari na ima di sktiwas we daka skpawan we sisin mrengo mesa kesaun ku na manu di.
S.TRANSL: 有些動物這樣建議。族老是說是繡眼畫眉。教我的老人是這麼說的。已故的Tiwas和Pawan告訴我：「是繡眼畫眉說的。」

It was proposed by some animals. Elderly people said that was thrush. Elderly people who taught me told me this. The elderly people who taught me said so. The late Tiwas and Pawan said to me, “It was thrush who said so.”

S.FORM: yamu seediq na ado namu uka gaya mesa kika so nii ka ga namu rih namu so ga qrinucun na ka utux a utux namu di mesa lm mesa ka kari na.
S.TRANSL: （繡眼畫眉）說：「你們賽德克人，因為你們沒有族律所以你們被神懲罰，你們的神。」他們說。
(Thrush said,) “You Seediq people, because you do not have tribal laws, you are punished by God, your God.” They said.

S.FORM: ma asi ka huwa kesaun ka so nii di mesa seediq ma kika s s lmngelung so nii ka sisin.
S.TRANSL: 「人這樣的情況，該怎麼辦才好？」繡眼畫眉這樣想。
“How to deal with this situation of people?” thrush throught like this.

S.FORM: ma ma lmngelung ka seediq ciida we o maha malu uri mesa.
S.TRANSL: 當時祖先們想著，然後說：「也好。」
At that time, ancestors thought, and said, “That is good.”

S.FORM: kika peeyah ciida kana cmcamac de srriyux mosa manu di mkmusa mkdmidin tasin btunux kii wa tasin paru wa kiya wa.
S.TRANSL: 之後，所有的動物輪流前去，想要搬那顆大石頭。
Afterwards, all the animals took turning going, wanting to move that big stone.

S.FORM: ma uka kmmekun.
S.TRANSL: 但沒人做到。
And no one could make it.

S.FORM: ma ani naq ani naq manu wa ani naq a ani naq so kesaun ta qbheni peni ani ta naq kesaun so camac sepac papak wa ani kerus ani ta naq kesaun qbheni eniqan palic peni musa uka kmmekun.
S.TRANSL: 不論是哪個，就算是我們所稱的四隻腳動物，就算是我們所稱有翅膀的鳥類，都去但沒人能做到。
No matter which one, even our so-called four-footed animals or the winged birds, they all went but could not make it.

S.FORM: ma bobo na de msngari teru teru bale ka manu di a camac gaga ini usa manu di a ququ dmidin dungan.
S.TRANSL: 然後，還剩下三個沒去試著搬。
Then, there remained three who did not go and try to move (the stone).

S.FORM: ini u ma kika kingan we e teru camac nii we kingan we a sisin ma kingan we pucaq ma kingan kingan ge manu di tiyaqung.
S.TRANSL: 這三個裡面，一個是繡眼畫眉，一個是紅嘴黑鵯，另一個是烏鴉。
Of these three, one was thrush; another was black bulbul; the other was crow.

S.FORM: ma ado paru ka tiyaqung na heya plaale musa sa tiyaqung.
S.TRANSL: 由於烏鴉的體型大，所以他先去。
Since crow was big, he went first.

S.FORM: wada tiyaqung musa dmidin btunux kii we.
S.TRANSL: 烏鴉去搬這塊石頭。
Crow went to move this stone.

S.FORM: ma inu dung wada wa wada iriqan papak ka ima di tiyaqung rmengac qduriq ini kmekun.
S.TRANSL: 而且，烏鴉的腳被壓到，他慘叫逃走。他沒做到。
What’s more, crow’s foot was hit and he yelled in pain and ran away. He could not make it.

S.FORM: ma kingan we mhedu kii de rih so msngari pucaq daka sisin di.
S.TRANSL: 那個結束後，表示剩下紅嘴黑鵯和繡眼畫眉。
That one was finished, which means there remained black bulbul and thrush.

S.FORM: wada ka pucaq duri.
S.TRANSL: 紅嘴黑鵯也去（試看看）。
Black bulbul also tried.

S.FORM: wada ka pucaq han mesa ka lmngelung ka sisin a yamu plaale mesa kesaun na pucaq un si pucaq wada puca wada pucaq ma.
S.TRANSL: 紅嘴黑鵯先去。繡眼畫眉想一下，說：「你們先去。」紅嘴黑鵯說「好」然後走了。
Black bulbul went first. Sisin thought and said, “You first.” Black bulbul said “yes” and went.

S.FORM: ani asi m kweko pucaq ini kmekun dmidin.
S.TRANSL: 不論紅嘴黑鵯如何使力都無法搬動。
No matter how hard black bulbul tried, he could not move (the stone).

S.FORM: ma kika bobo na de m rih so msngari ano wano sis m mtngari wano sisin ini usa dmidin di.
S.TRANSL: 然後，就只剩繡眼畫眉沒去搬。
Then, there remained only sisin who did not go and move.

S.FORM: ciida seediq a seediq ta mesa ka rudan a ima sktiwas we naqah kuxun daha bale naqah kuxun daha sa maanu bale nii di.
S.TRANSL: 已故的Tiwas說當時的人很傷心，說：「這樣我們該如何是好？」
The late Tiwas said that people at that time were really sad, saying “What can we do with this?”

S.FORM: mpkmekun ba maanu qbheni biciq nii mesa ka lmngelung ka ima di seediq si peni na.
S.TRANSL: 人們想：「這麼小的鳥，有什麼能耐呢？」
People thought, “What is such a small bird capable of doing?”

S.FORM: ma kana cmcamc uri we mesa ma misa maha uka kmekun mangan btunux dmidin btunux kii di mesa lmngelung.
S.TRANSL: 所有的動物也認為或許沒人能搬移那顆石頭。
All the animals as well thought probably no one was capable of taking and carrying that stone.

S.FORM: ma inu sisin nii ini kesai mmaanu wada sisin wada musa a kesaun na dgiyaq a pttingan kii.
S.TRANSL: 但繡眼畫眉什麼都沒說。他前去那座所謂的Pttingan山。
But thrush did not say anything. He went to that so-called Pttingan mountain.

S.FORM: ma kika didilun na inu dungan ini qbsiyaq dilan daha bale btunux kiya.
S.TRANSL: 沒多久他們抬起那顆石頭。
It was not long before they raised that stone.

S.FORM: ma so hari ka ga mita utux di ma so hari manu di e wada mklui paru peni.
S.TRANSL: 就像是見到了神一般，也像是受到極大的驚嚇。
It was like they saw the God. It was also like they were frightened greatly.

S.FORM: kedu beyax na sisin nii si kika ma mqaras riyung kana seediq di.
S.TRANSL: 人們說：「這繡眼畫眉怎麼那麼厲害！」他們全都很高興。
People said, “How powerful thrush is!” They are all very happy.

S.FORM: kiya ka wada angalun kndalax ciida ka wada angalun gaya kesaun ita seediq ka sisin nii.
S.TRANSL: 所以，從那時候起，我們賽德克人就以繡眼畫眉的話作為族律。
Thus, from then on, our Seediq people take thrush’s words as tribal law.

S.FORM: ma bobo na na si e ma mesa ka seediq dung ciida eniqan ka kingan kari dungan mesa.
S.TRANSL: 之後，人又說當時有另一個故事。
Afterwards, people say there was another story at that time.

S.FORM: pawan ka manu di rnengo tikuh nii da mesa ka skpawan skpawan nawi wa.
S.TRANSL: Pawan這個說了一些。已故的Pawan Nawi說的。
Pawan said a little about this. The late Pawan Nawi told about it.

S.FORM: ke ke a mesa te m uxe uxe kingan ba kesaun na sktiwas daha btunux ga hii kesaun na.
S.TRANSL: 是這樣的：「真的不只是一顆，而是有兩顆石頭在那裡。」他告訴已故的Tiwas。
Here it goes, “There were not really one, but two stones over there,” he told the late Tiwas.

S.FORM: o mesa tiwas ma hmuwa kesaun na ma.
S.TRANSL: Tiwas說：「喔？然後又怎麼了？」
Tiwas said, ‘Oh? Then what happened?’

S.FORM: mesa ka ma pawan ka smneeru nii di kesaun ku na ma ciida na kela naq wisu egu riyung ka cmcamac ini skela qmita ka dnidin daha a maanu di a dnidin daha b musa dmidin berah a s musa dmidin a a maanu di btunux paru mgaya kii we ini skela kii ka duma camac dehuk hiya kika smiling a sisin dungan o a pulu hawan bale ha ma miyan ini skela ma miyan ini skela ka yami di mesa peni.
S.TRANSL: 有些動物沒趕上（目睹他們）第一次去搬那顆大石頭，到了那邊，又問繡眼畫眉，後悔說：「為什麼我們錯過了？為什麼我們錯過了？」
Some animals who failed to (witness them) go to move that big stone for the first time arrived there and asked thrush again, regretting, “Why did we miss it? Why did we miss it?”

S.FORM: kika wada inu dungan kesaun na sisin te e hmuwa ka musa namu mangan texan dungan ado miyan so kelaun mesa isu isu bale ka mu mu mnadis btunux nii kesaun na ka sisin ma.
S.TRANSL: 因此他們去向繡眼畫眉說：「你們再去拿回一顆石頭如何？好讓我們知道這顆石頭是你們搬過來的。」他們跟繡眼畫眉這麼說。
Thus they went and said to thrush, “How about you going again to bring back a huge stone, so that we know it was really you who have brought back this stone?” they said so to thrush.

S.FORM: ini kesai mmaanu ka sisin wada dungan.
S.TRANSL: 繡眼畫眉不置可否，又去了。
Thrush said nothing and left again.

S.FORM: ana dehuk hndure hari rudan na musa mesa ka pawan nii ani dehuk a hndure hari rudan m musa maanu di tmlenga hii di na odaun daha mita btunux nii di na m rih so u o nii a btunux dnidin tasin kesaun s kesaun na ka lmutuc kari ka laqi betaq saya kesaun ku na.
S.TRANSL: Pawan說，即使到最近有些老人家到那裡放陷阱，他們經過這個石頭的時候說：「應該要告訴小孩這個故事。」他告訴我。
Pawan said that recently some elderly went there to set traps. When thet passed this stone, they said, “It is necessary that chidren be told this story,” he told me.

S.FORM: ma meya ado uxe miyan mekan meluq mesa ka seediq smiling sisin peni.
S.TRANSL: 「是不是因為我們吃生的食物？」他們問繡眼畫眉。
“Is this because we eat raw food?” people asked thrush.

S.FORM: m malu uri wa mesa sisin ma kika sisin de heya de lmngelung.
S.TRANSL: 「嗯，那也好。」繡眼畫眉說，思考著。
“Well, that would be good,” thrush said and thought.

S.FORM: ma p splawa na ka ima di tiyaqung daka ima di pucaq peni.
S.TRANSL: 然後，他叫烏鴉和紅嘴黑鵯過來。
Then, he called crow and black bulbul to come over.

S.FORM: a kesai kesao ta di ho naka su musa skadi maanu di irih irih so pusu puniq peni.
S.TRANSL: 「我們這樣做好嗎？你是否可以去找那個…應該是火的根源？」
“What do you say we do this? Can you go find... the supposed source of fire?”

S.FORM: huwa kesaun rmengo saya ini ku kela uri.
S.TRANSL: 現在怎麼稱呼這個東西？我也不知道。
How to call it now? I do not know, either.

S.FORM: o usa skadi a pusu puniq mesa ka sisin a skagulan na ka ima di a tiyaqung daka a tucaq nii.
S.TRANSL: 「去找火源。」繡眼畫眉說，派遣烏鴉和紅嘴黑鵯。
“Go find the source of fire,” thrush said and dispatched crow and black bulbul.

S.FORM: ma ma lmngelung pucaq daka tiyaqung nii we kedu bale beyax na sisin nii asi thei.
S.TRANSL: 烏鴉和紅嘴黑鵯想到繡眼畫眉那麼的厲害，於是立刻聽話。
Crow and Black bulbul thought of such power thrush had, so they obeyed immediately.

S.FORM: adoka km kmmekun dmidin btunux paru heya na ma lmngelung naq sisin daka tiyaqung nii uri o ma nii si ma niqa ma ga dayoan utux ka heya mesa lmngelung ma asi thei ka pucaq daka ima di tiyaqung nii.
S.TRANSL: 因繡眼畫眉能搬移大石頭，紅嘴黑鵯和烏鴉自己想，他是受到神的幫助。因此紅嘴黑鵯和烏鴉立刻答應。
Because thrush can move the big stone, black bulbul and crow thought to themselves, he is helped by God. Thus black bulbul and crow obeyed immediately.

S.FORM: kika wada wada mquri hunac skadi kesaun a pusu a pusu puniq.
S.TRANSL: 然後他們就往下游去尋找所謂的火源。
Then they went downstream to find the so-called source of fire.

S.FORM: ma yani wada bsiyaq hari wa ma kika meyah.
S.TRANSL: 過了一段時間，（他們）回來了。
After a while, (they) came back.

S.FORM: qtaan de gisu henu gisu ka deheya nii a pucaq daka maanu tiyaqung nii ma mgeela ka pucaq ma qitaan daha pucaq kii na mgtanah ka quwaq na mgtanah kana papak na.
S.TRANSL: 他們看到紅嘴黑鵯和那烏鴉，紅嘴黑鵯在前。他們看到紅嘴黑鵯的嘴巴紅紅的，腳也紅紅的。
They saw black bulbul and crow, and black bulbul was in the front. They saw black bulbul’s mouth was reddish, and his feet were reddish, too.

S.FORM: ma pu a p ma menaq ka tiyaqung we qa qa smdurak pucaq kndalax bukuy ka heya.
S.TRANSL: 而烏鴉嘎嘎地叫，追在紅嘴黑鵯之後。
And crow was cawing, following at the back of black bulbul.

S.FORM: a mtanah kii wa so hari mtanah maanu ini kelai ptutingun na dheran.
S.TRANSL: 他把那紅色不知道是什麼的東西放落到地上。
He dropped that unknown red thing on the ground.

S.FORM: ma inu di ptutingun na dheran ciida na kii naq malu karac ciida tnugiyo eniqan ka so sudu mnddengu menaq dheran na asi sqama.
S.TRANSL: 哎呀！他放落到地上。那時候是晴天，地上都是乾草，馬上燒起來。
Oh dear! He dropped (it) on the ground. At that time, it was sunny, and all over the ground there were dry grass, which burned immediately.

S.FORM: ma kika s sqama slamaq ka kika m a so sudu mnddengu kiya ma maanu nii su qduriq seediq.
S.TRANSL: 然後那乾枯的草在燃燒。「這是什麼？」人們逃開。
Then that dry grass was burning. “What is it?” people ran away.

S.FORM: ma so kiya hari de mt wada wada mthedu su slamaq sqama de uka di uka mtanah kii di ma mbhege di.
S.TRANSL: 燃燒結束後，那不再是紅色。它變成白色。
As the burning stopped, that was not red anymore, It become white.

S.FORM: qitaan we so hari btunux mbhege ma so hari a maanu di qrari qrari peni.
S.TRANSL: 看起來像白色的石頭，白燧石。
It looked like a white stone, a white flint.

S.FORM: ma btunux kii de so hari bhege ma qrari ma saadux qitaan.
S.TRANSL: 那石頭看起來像是白色且硬的白晶石。
That stone looked like a white and hard flint.

S.FORM: ma e maanu ma wada inu ma uka puniq di mesa ka seediq ma.
S.TRANSL: 然後有人說：「火跑去哪裡了？」
Then someone said, “Where has the fire gone?”

S.FORM: ye ma eniqan kingan seediq um wada ini ini rnrengo ka kingan seediq wada musa hii ma angalun na ka daha btunux kiya kika king daha btunux nadis na ima di pucaq nii na qn qniyucan na kingan ma kika qeraqan na papak kingan.
S.TRANSL: 然後有一個人走到那裡，一聲不響拿起那兩顆石頭，那兩顆紅嘴黑鵯帶回來的石頭，一顆咬在嘴裡，另一顆用腳抓著。

Then, there was one man who went there without saying anything, and picked up those two stones, the two stones that black bulbul had brought back, one he bit in his mouth, and the other he grabbed by his feet.

S.FORM: kika s angalun na kndalax dheran na ma kika putu maanu di rih so putungan na.
S.TRANSL: 他從地上撿起撿起，然後將（兩顆燧石）相互擦擊點火。
He picked (them) up from the ground and then he lit the fire (by rubbing them against each other).

S.FORM: putungan na ma ey ma malux malu malux eniqan a rana puniq mesa ka ima di seediq nii peni wa kela naq so nii mesa.
S.TRANSL: 敲擊時，這個人說：「怎麼有火花？」「原來是這樣啊」他說。
When knocking, “why there is fire spark?” this man said. “It was like this,” he said.

S.FORM: o kela naq so nii kiya kiya nii na mesa ka ima di seediq ma mqaras bale ka seediq kedu su bale knmalu kesaun na ka pucaq nii peni.
S.TRANSL: 「原來是這樣啊，」人們說。人們很開心。「你真的很好，」人們對紅嘴黑鵯說。
“So it works this way,” people said. People were really happy. “You are really kind,” they said to black bulbul.

S.FORM: ma menaq ka kesaun menaq a pucaq lmiqu nii na saya di na pucaq lmiqu mesa kari miyan laqi da rudan na pucaq mesa.
S.TRANSL: 此外，有關這紅嘴黑鵯，現在年輕人以我們族語稱為Pucaq lmiqu，而長者們稱之為Pucaq。
Besides, with regard to this black bulbul, now younger people call them Pucaq lmiqu in our language, while elderly people call them Pucaq.

S.FORM: malux ka tanah quwaq na daha papak na na.
S.TRANSL: 何以他的嘴和腳是紅色的呢？
Why are his beak and feet red?

S.FORM: rih so wada wada hu uxe kesaun wada slamaqun so hari wada slamaqun maanu di rtahuc puniq kika maanu di quwaq na daka papak na ma kika mtanah papak na daka quwaq na pucaq lmiqu dehuk saya mesa ka rudan.
S.TRANSL: 也就是說，紅嘴黑鵯似乎是那時候被餘火燒，所以到現在嘴跟腳都是紅的，族老說。
In other words, black bulbul seemed to be burned by the ember, so his mouth and feet are still red now, the elderly said.

S.FORM: ma menaq ka menaq ka tiyaqung ga na kingan kari daha we wada iriqun btunux kesaun su mu ini daha nkmekuli a dmidin soni na.
S.TRANSL: 那麼，至於烏鴉呢，有一種說法。我剛才告訴你他被石頭壓到，沒辦法搬動石頭。
Then, as for crow, there is one story. I just told you he was hit by the stone and could not move the stone.

S.FORM: musa musa skadi maanu di a pusu puniq nii pucaq daka maanu di a tiyaqung nii na.
S.TRANSL: 紅嘴黑鵯和烏鴉去找火源。
Black bulbul and crow went to find the source of fire.

S.FORM: ado plaale mang ado plaale a maanu di smdiyan ka tiyaqung a maanu di pucaq kika ma mesa mesa ka tiyaqung lngelungan na mesa a ma uxe kii di ma ta na uxe baka dungan peni.
S.TRANSL: 因為紅嘴黑鵯先找到，所以烏鴉想著：「我們怎麼可以又輸給紅嘴黑鵯？」
Because black bulbul found it first, crow thought, “How can we lose to him again?”

S.FORM: kika osaun na gmeeluk inu naq dung knk kmekulun su gmeeluk maanu ka pucaq a.
S.TRANSL: 所以烏鴉去搶紅嘴黑鵯，但是你怎能搶得過紅嘴黑鵯呢？
Thus he went to rob black bulbul, but how could you possibly rob black bulbul?

S.FORM: kii naq beyax na ps maanu di so ps kmetu ma dmoi papak na uka na ngangalan.
S.TRANSL: 可能紅嘴黑鵯咬得用力也抓得很用力，所以烏鴉沒辦法拿走。
Black bulbul may have made effort to bite and grab, so crow could not take it away.

S.FORM: ma musa hu hmeri heya musa musa lux maanu di pculing hii ka ima di tiyaqung nii di.
S.TRANSL: 烏鴉反而自己無故讓火源給燙傷。
Instead, crow went and got scalded for no reason.

S.FORM: kika dehuk dheran tiyaqung nii na ado ka pnculing ka menaq maanu di skiya baro han na ado pnculing papak na peni kika dehuk dheran di na kika so hari sbbobo icin kkesa na ani dehuk saya tiyaqung nii di so kika kari rudan ta.
S.TRANSL: 因此，當烏鴉到達地面時，因為他的腳在上面飛行的時候被燙傷，所以直到今日走路起來還是搖搖晃晃的。我們的先祖是這麼傳述的。
Thus, when crow arrived on the ground, because his feet were scalded when he flew above, he swayed when walking even to this day. Our ancestors told the story like this.

S.FORM: ma ki ma kiya ani dehuk saya na kesaun tongan nii na kari kesaun tongan nii na putungan k pusu na kari we putungan.
S.TRANSL: 即使到今天，那被稱做Tongan的地方，所說的Tongan這個字，詞源就是p-utung-an（擦擊點火）。
And even to this day, the place called Tongan, the so-called Tongan this word, its word root is p-utung-an.

S.FORM: zi bale bale na e kesaun pu a pkesaun a tongan nii na putungan bale ba ka ngayan na.
S.TRANSL: 事實上，Tongan這個地方，他的名字原來真的是ptungan。
In fact, Tongan this place, its name was really ptungan.

S.FORM: ma bale ba uri ga inu saya kesaun na qra qrari naq knmalu kiya sn snlayan pucaq kii ini ku kela uri.
S.TRANSL: 這是真的嗎？紅嘴黑鵯找到好的白燧石的地方在哪裡？我也不知道。
Was it true? Where is the real place where black bulbul found the nice white flints? I don’t know, either.

S.FORM: kiya naq dehuk hini kari mu.
S.TRANSL: 就這樣，我的話說到這裡。
That’s all. My words end here.
